mnemonics.title: CK Equation Magic Square
mnemonics.content: P^(n+m) = P^n × P^m — Think of 'CK' as 'Counting Klicks' where you multiply steps: n clicks + m clicks = total clicks (n+m). The probability matrix for combined steps equals the matrix product of individual step matrices.

mnemonics.title: TRAnts vs RECUrRents
mnemonics.content: TRANSIENT states are like tourists — they visit briefly and may NEVER return. RECURRENT (persistent) states are like your HOME — you always come BACK. Remember: 'Tourists Never Return, Residents Always Come Back.'

mnemonics.title: Aperiodic Absorbs Periodicity
mnemonics.content: APERIODIC = 'A' (no) PERIOD (regular interval). An aperiodic state can return at ANY time (no fixed cycle). ABSORBING states are 'ABsorbent' — they soak up all probability and let nothing escape (can't leave once entered).

acronyms.usage: Use when calculating n-step transition probabilities in Markov chains. Formula: P^(n+m) = P^n × P^m, where P is the transition matrix.
Use to find probability of moving from state i to state j in exactly n steps. Entry (i,j) gives P(X_n = j | X_0 = i).
Use when calculating expected number of steps to reach a state for the first time. Related to mean recurrence time.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a grid of rooms (states). To find probability of going from Room A to Room C in 5 steps, picture: Room A → [intermediate rooms in 2 steps] → [remaining rooms in 3 steps]. Multiply the 2-step matrix by the 3-step matrix — like connecting two chains of rooms end-to-end.

Draw a state diagram with arrows. Transient states have arrows leading OUT but NO path returning back (like one-way streets leading to a dead end). Recurrent states have paths leading BACK to themselves (like roundabouts you can always return to).

Think of a clock face. If a state is periodic with period 3, you can only return at hours 3, 6, 9, 12... Draw a circle with only 3 marked points. Aperiodic is a circle with ALL points marked — you can return anytime.
Picture a quicksand pit or black hole — once you enter, you cannot escape. Draw an arrow pointing INTO the state with NO arrows coming OUT. The self-loop probability = 1.

analogies.analogy: Think of train journeys. To find probability of traveling from Delhi to Mumbai via Jaipur in 2+3=5 hours: multiply the Delhi→Jaipur route probability by Jaipur→Mumbai route probability. The total journey probability is the product of segment probabilities.
Like a bus stop on a one-way route — you might stop there once, but if you continue, you may never return. Some passengers get off and leave forever (probability of returning < 1).
Like your hometown — no matter how far you travel, you're guaranteed to come back eventually. The probability of returning is 1, and the expected return time is finite.
Like graduating from university — once you enter the 'graduated' state, you cannot go back to being a student. It's a permanent state with 100% probability of staying.

A periodic state is like a seasonal business — customers only return during certain seasons (e.g., ice cream shops in summer). An aperiodic state is like a grocery store — customers return anytime, no fixed schedule.
